organizacion.descripcion: An interdisciplinary educational organization that integrates psychology, performing arts, and social sciences to generate formative experiences connecting body, mind, and society. More than just courses, it creates spaces for research, community, and human transformation.

filosofia.lema_fundamental: We believe that the body, the mind, and the social environment cannot be understood in isolation.

organizacion.proposito: We build community through shared knowledge and bridge the gap between theory and experience, turning introspection into movement and movement into social awareness to generate deep human development with real impact.
organizacion.metodologia: We rely on critical thinking, research, and art as real tools for human and collective transformation.

diplomado.aval: Endorsed by the Benemérita Universidad Autónoma de Puebla (BUAP)

informacion_general.horarios: Saturdays from 9:00 am to 1:00 pm (Mexico City Time)
sedes: LEACT Dance Studio (Tragaluz Artistic and Cultural Experimentation Laboratory)

perfil_egreso.vision_general: Training professionals with an interdisciplinary and critical vision, capable of understanding well-being as a collective process linked to the social context. It develops the ability to design intervention, research, or creation projects using art as a tool for resistance, memory, community care, and transformation with true cultural impact.

enfoques_por_disciplina.disciplina: The Graduate from a Psychology Perspective
enfoques_por_disciplina.resumen: Takes clinical practice out of the traditional consulting room and into social reality. It allows understanding the psyche from a socio-cultural environment, de-pathologizing suffering through artistic tools and an ethical approach to human rights and collective well-being.

enfoques_por_disciplina.disciplina: The Graduate from a Social Sciences Perspective
enfoques_por_disciplina.resumen: Brings critical theory directly down to the territory and the body. It provides creative methodologies to co-create projects with communities, design interdisciplinary well-being strategies, and activate spaces of resistance against system inequalities.

enfoques_por_disciplina.disciplina: The Graduate from a Performing Arts Perspective
enfoques_por_disciplina.resumen: Provides the creative process with a rigorous political, social, and methodological sense, ideal for international grant applications. It allows using the body as a vehicle for memory and collaborating horizontally with psychology or sociology in collective care laboratories.

modulos.titulo: Integral Well-being, Subjectivity, and Critical Thinking
clases: Welcome and introduction
Integral well-being
Identity, crisis, and human development
Critical theory and art

modulos.titulo: Colonialism, Production of Otherness, and Resistance
clases: Understanding Otherness for professional practice
The coloniality of knowledge: questioning Eurocentric psychology and arts
Arts and representations: The male colonial gaze in aesthetics
Decolonizing knowledge, feeling, and doing through art
Practical Laboratory 1: Decolonial methodologies and tools for artistic, psychological, and social intervention.

modulos.titulo: Capitalism and Resistances from a Care Perspective
clases: Understanding capitalism: accumulation, power, and inequality
Mental health and class: The psychologization and pathologization of precariousness
Bodily and psychosomatic systems: sensitive and healthy bodies vs. the productive body.
Rethinking work from a care perspective
Practical Laboratory 2: Self-management strategies and support networks

modulos.titulo: Patriarchy, Gender, and the Production of Subjectivities
clases: Understanding gender: the history of feminism
Feminist geopolitics as an analytical methodology: from the global to the personal
Gender violence in Mexico: implications for mental health
Bodies, identities, and sexual diversity
Practical Laboratory 3: Intervention and creation tools with a gender perspective / Follow-up and final project proposals
Specialized lecture series
Application workshop: development of critical, comprehensive, and interdisciplinary projects
Seminary: the role of the graduate in current integral well-being
Group mentorship and project follow-up
1st Dress rehearsal for presentations, performing pieces, projects, and final feedback
2nd Dress rehearsal for presentations, performing pieces, projects, and final feedback
Final projects colloquium

evento_cierre.descripcion: This is the program's closing event, designed to function simultaneously as an academic convention and a community arts festival. For one week, traditional exams are set aside to open a living space where you can share the projects, reflections, and performing proposals developed in class, as well as connect with local and international guests and research. Through activities such as talks, workshops, exhibitions, and artistic presentations, this gathering is the key moment to take knowledge out of the notebook and see how it transforms into real creation and collective celebration—an integral well-being approach that builds community.

inscripcion.descripcion: Mandatory initial payment required to secure a spot and enable access to financing schemes and tuition payment plans.

detalles_adicionales.modalidad_stripe: The registration payment IS credited toward the BUAP certificate fee ($2,200 MXN), so you would only pay $700 MXN for that concept.
detalles_adicionales.modalidad_transferencia_efectivo: The registration payment IS NOT credited toward the BUAP certificate fee ($2,200 MXN).

politicas.regla: Monthly tuition payments must be made strictly from the 14th to the 18th of each month to maintain the assigned scholarship benefit.
Starting on the 19th of each month, an automatic 8% late fee will be applied to the regular payment amount.
The accumulation of three late payments will result in the total and permanent cancellation of the awarded scholarship, requiring the student to cover the regular cost.
The cost of the BUAP certificate is $2,200 MXN (subject to registration discount conditions depending on the chosen payment gateway).

maestros.perfil: Choreographer, performer, educator, and philologist. Awarded the National Culture Prize in Dance 2024, his work intertwines creation, research, and the decoloniality of the body. He is co-director of the collectives Yunta and Reinas Arte Escénico. Through laboratories like his workshop 'Maleable', he proposes enhancing an 'extra-everyday scenic body', sharing his methodology across Costa Rica, Guatemala, and Mexico.

General Director and Founder of Human MooveArts. Psychologist from BUAP, Bachelor in Dance from UDLAP, and Master's candidate in Artistic-Creative Therapies. She has training in psychotherapy, psychological counseling, and clinical skills. With over 15 years of performing and teaching experience, she has built an important bridge between mental health and movement arts. Her work spans teaching at all academic levels, choreographic research, and leadership in community intervention projects. Her participation in artistic and vulnerable sector projects outside Mexico includes Costa Rica, Guatemala, and New York. Her pedagogy merges integrative psychology and bodily praxis under a deep conviction: 'In a world full of violence, tenderness is revolution.'

Scenic artist, educator, creator, and philologist in English literature. Their artistic and investigative praxis is nurtured by theater directing, performance, and dissident spoken word. With an approach deeply rooted in decolonial, gender, and bodily narratives, they have shared their work and presentations in international spaces across Mexico, El Salvador, Kenya, and the United States, utilizing scenic art as a territory for resistance, memory, and social transformation.

Multidisciplinary artist, researcher, writer, and facilitator. Her practice pioneeringly intertwines movement arts, somatic education, neuroscience, and ecology. With a solid international background in Sweden, Spain, and Denmark, Laura investigates psycho-corporal processes in natural and aquatic environments through her own methodologies like 'Danzar el Paisaje' (Dancing the Landscape). Her pedagogical work approaches kinesis from ecofeminist and comprehensive well-being perspectives, facilitating laboratories in Latin America and Europe.

Specialist in political communication, branding, digital strategy, and public narrative construction. Master in Public Opinion and Political Marketing from BUAP and graduate from UDLAP, he has a solid track record as a brand consultant, lecturer, and academic coordinator. His experience covers social and institutional communication management, linking verbal and non-verbal communication strategies to boost corporate identity and the development of projects with social and community impact.

Social psychologist, researcher, and professor. He is a PhD candidate in Psychological Research at ITESO and holds a Master's in Social Psychology from BUAP. His work specializes in qualitative research, discourse analysis, and the psychosocial approach to territory, public space, gender, and the cultural politics of emotions. He has participated in international research projects on social dynamics and is co-author of publications focused on moral judgments and social categorization in Latin America.

Choreographer, director, actor, educator, and psychologist from the University of Costa Rica (UCR). He holds an Academic Master's in Arts and is a PhD student in Society and Culture Studies (UCR). A pioneer and leader of Central American contemporary dance, he founded the iconic company Losdenmedium and the revolutionary Conservatorio El Barco. He has been awarded more than ten National Dance and Theater Prizes, and his innovative pedagogical approach based on the concepts of Play, Fiction, and Surprise has trained generations of creators. He is currently a university professor, international guest lecturer, and general director of the ENSIMISMADOS platform.

Internationalist, Master in Pedagogy (UPAEP), and Master's student in Gender Studies (IAP). She is a specialist in Human Rights, gender perspective, violence prevention, and educational policies, with international research stays and presentations in the US, Spain, Colombia, and Ecuador. She has collaborated with the Secretariat of Education and the Secretariat of Substantive Equality of Puebla as a facilitator of reflection circles and curriculum designer. Activist and co-founder of the Colectiva Kindaa, her work focuses on the collective construction of the city, public space, and community education from a feminist approach.

Psychologist, sociologist, researcher, and lecturer with a humanistic and critical perspective. She holds a PhD in Sociology from BUAP and has an outstanding career in university teaching (IBERO, BUAP, UPAEP) and in the coordination of social community-incidence projects. She has been responsible for the IBERO Puebla Community Center and has several publications focused on urban territorial issues, participatory methodologies, gender, space appropriation, and eco-technological alternatives in peripheral areas.

Internationalist (IBERO Puebla), researcher, and dancer dedicated to the defense of human rights and community building. With research lines in gender, geopolitics, and decoloniality, she has academic publications in the journals Incidencias and Contratiempo. She has trained in international dance platforms such as REVÉS (Costa Rica) and CONNATURAL (Guatemala); she currently collaborates with Mexiro A.C. on the national maternity law proposal. From activism and movement, she understands dance as a political act to question social structures and inhabit the body-territory.

Choreographer, video dance creator, educator, and international performer. She holds a Master of Fine Arts (M.F.A.) in Dance from Texas Woman's University and from Sung Kyun Kwan University in Seoul. She was a full-time professor and coordinator of the Dance program at UDLAP. Her interdisciplinary work, awarded in film and dance festivals in Asia, Europe, and America, investigates contact improvisation, traditional Korean dances, and site-specific work. She is co-founder of the artistic organization kNOwBOX Dance and programming director of the festival of the same name.

Choreographer, dancer, teacher, and artistic producer. Master in Dance with an emphasis on pedagogy from the National University of Costa Rica and a graduate of the prestigious Conservatorio El Barco. He was the Director of the National Dance Company of Costa Rica (2023-2025). His pedagogical proposal and movement research—materialized in international workshops such as Eyectado and Cyborg—focuses on bodily autonomy, horizontality, and subjectivity in movement. He is co-founder of the international platform ENSIMISMADOS and the collective Reinas Arte Escénico, weaving a solid track record of creation in university networks and festivals throughout Latin America.

Psychologist from BUAP, she holds a double Master's degree in Psychoanalytic Psychotherapy (Centro Eleia) and in Pedagogy (UPAEP), in addition to Doctoral studies in Clinical Psychoanalysis. With over 8 years of experience, she is a specialist in institutional mental health, organizational well-being, and educational psychology. She has designed and implemented crisis intervention, psychological containment, and psychosocial risk prevention programs in large-scale educational contexts. Her experience includes working as Senior Manager at Fundación Azteca and Department Head at the SEP of Puebla, as well as multidisciplinary collaboration with UNICEF in emergency care. She holds an official certification from CONOCER for attending to female victims of violence, consolidating an integrative vision that joins clinical rigor with contemporary psychopedagogical management.

Musician, flutist, and music educator specialized in applied music therapy for education and psychomotor stimulation. With a solid background in neurosciences and inclusive music pedagogies, she has shared her research in international forums and congresses in Cuba and Brazil. Her practice intertwines chamber music, musical theater, and the study of neurodiversity, exploring sound and rhythm as fundamental tools for holistic human development and well-being.

Electronic and Communications Engineer with an MBA in Services Marketing from UDLAP. He is a specialist in stage technology, lighting design, and videomapping. He has a career spanning over two decades in technical direction, logistics, and the execution of hundreds of theater, dance, opera staging, and international festivals like Performatica. His experience includes working as Crew Chief for Cirque du Soleil's Joya show and his current management as Coordinator of Performing Arts Productions at UDLAP, uniquely fusing engineering, teaching, and artistic sensitivity.

Primary Education Teacher, career civil servant, and Head of Studies specialized in educational innovation, active methodologies, and teaching digital competence. With an outstanding career in pedagogical coordination and the design of inclusive learning situations, his work focuses on the didactic integration of ICT, artificial intelligence applied to education, educational robotics, and school radio as a competence tool. A collaborator in pedagogical innovation groups, he stands out for his focus on designing learning spaces and creating motivating educational resources connected to the community.

Social educator, child and youth trauma therapist, and specialist in vulnerability contexts. She holds a Diploma in Systemic Child and Youth Trauma Therapy. Her professional and volunteer career focuses on socio-educational intervention with minors in the protection system, accompaniment in development and learning processes, and conflict management. She is an expert in the detection and treatment of early trauma, abuse, and attachment dynamics, promoting a clinical and educational practice with a deeply healing approach.

Scenic artist, social communicator, dance manager, and activist. Specialized in urban dances with a 10-year career on the national and international scene, she has represented Mexico in dance battle festivals in countries like Colombia and has trained in prominent companies in the country. Her work as a choreographer fuses movement with activism, creating stagings focused on mental health, gender equity, and the visibility of women in collaboration with artists like Audry Funk. She is the founder and driver of initiatives aimed at creating safe spaces in the dance environment.

Clinical Psychologist (Cum Laude) with a diploma in Neuroscience (URL), dancer, and psycho-corporal therapist. Specialized in the 'Terapia de Reencuentro' (Reencounter Therapy) methodology and Authentic Movement, she has a solid international track record accompanying trauma survivors, youth at risk, and in art therapy processes. She is a scenic researcher, winner of the Iberescena prize and the Bienal en Resistencia, and co-founder of the prominent Central American platform CONNATURAL. As a teacher and facilitator, she weaves deep psychology with Contact Improvisation for collective transformation and healing.

Psychologist, therapist, researcher, and current Director of the Faculty of Psychology at the Benemérita Universidad Autónoma de Puebla (BUAP) for the 2024-2028 period. Doctor in Psychological Research, he has served as Coordinator of the Master's degrees in Psychotherapy and in Neuropsychological Diagnosis and Rehabilitation at the same institution. His management and lines of research focus on holistic well-being, peace culture, psychopathology, and personality, standing out for his push towards postgraduate excellence and the creation of new curricula with social impact.

Communicator, music producer, and sound curator with more than 15 years of experience in the media, entertainment, and digital well-being industries. He holds a Bachelor's degree in Communication Sciences from the University of León and is the founder of Subconscious Alchemy, an innovative audio brand focused on well-being that merges music production with principles of neuroplasticity, binaural frequencies, and nervous system stimulation. A specialist in sonic identity and sensory branding, his work explores the power of audio design and music curation as tools for transformation, healing, and psycho-physical balance.

Activist, public policy consultant, communicator, and poet with more than 25 years of experience defending human rights. He is an advisory council member of the CDH Puebla, a board member of the CEEAVI, and Director of Communication at Odesyr AC. A specialist in designing educational models regarding gender, masculinities, and sexual rights, his work intertwines the rigor of social sciences with the power of art. He is the winner of the Letras Poblanas 2025 Prize in Poetry and a historical reference in cultural management and community activism in the region.

testimonios.resumen: A deeply transformative experience that invites questioning certainties about the body, care, and community. It highlights the richness of sharing with different disciplines and the value of collectivity and listening to develop a more critical, sensitive, and human professional practice.

The diploma program boosts professional growth by opening new paths to apply knowledge for the benefit of others. On a personal level, bodily and movement practice allows releasing emotions, guided by teachers capable of transmitting knowledge, hope, and change.

An incredible program with top-tier information, activities, and professors. It engages participants as individuals, managing to create authentic bonds through shared experiences. It is a true hub for growth, deep learning, and community.

Highly valuable for questioning with historical and social context the harsh attitudes normalized in art teaching. It offers bodily exploration tools that enrich the understanding of movement and emotional expression, being very useful even for other visual areas such as animation.

Ideal to complement dance teaching from a conscious psychological and social perspective. The diploma program provides practical tools that go beyond theory; they are lived in the body and transform personal, professional, and gender processes.

contacto.manifiesto_invitacion: Art and movement are not privileges for a few; they are tools of resistance and transformation within reach of any body willing to inhabit them. At HUMAN MOOVEARTS, we give you the space, the critical theory, and the community; you just bring the intention. Don't think twice. The moment to connect your mind with collective experience, question your surroundings, and transform your reality through movement is now.
